As the president and co-founder of the Amer Foundation, an organization dedicated to help families of illegal detainees and educate on Middle East policy and geopolitics, she said is seeing some positive steps being taken including Lebanese President Joseph Aoun calling for negotiations with Israel.

She said the "only solution is to have peace with Israel. I think there a lot of Shiites who are against Hezbollah… The majority of the Lebanese people just want peace. We hope the Trump administration will push the Lebanese government and Israel’s government to start peace talks."

Last week, Iran’s regime defied Lebanon’s expulsion order for its ambassador by saying he would stay, further increasing tensions in a country in the crosshairs of the latest fighting between the Iranian-backed Hezbollah and Israel.

Lebanon had declared Ambassador Mohammad Reza Shibani "persona non grata" to weaken Iran’s diplomatic presence and have a chargé d'affaires at its embassy instead. But the deadline to leave the country was Sunday and an Iranian spokesperson said the ambassador’s mission in Beirut continues.
CLICK HERE TO DOWNLOAD THE FOX NEWS APP
Fox News Digital reached out to Lebanon’s government and the Embassy in Washington D.C. for a comment.
The Associated Press and Reuters contributed to this report.
Benjamin Weinthal reports on Israel, Iran, Syria, Turkey and Europe. You can follow Benjamin on Twitter @BenWeinthal, and email him at benjamin.weinthal@fox.com

title: UK's NATO show of force ends with docked destroyer in Mediterranean after 'technical' issue
summary: The U.K. docked its sole naval warship in the Mediterranean due to technical issues amid growing criticism of PM Starmer's response to the Iran war.
story: Democrats blast President Donald Trump's two-week ceasefire deal with Iran, invoking the 25th Amendment and accusing him of war crimes. This showcases deep partisan division over Middle East diplomacy.

The United Kingdom’s only warship deployed to the eastern Mediterranean during the Iran conflict has been forced into port over a "technical" issue, abruptly sidelining a key piece of Britain’s regional military presence as pressure mounts on Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s handling of the crisis.

The docking of HMS Dragon — a Type 45 destroyer tasked with defending U.K. assets and projecting force near the conflict zone — weakens Britain’s visible military posture at a sensitive moment, as a fragile U.S.-brokered pause takes hold and criticism from Trump administration officials and conservative voices builds over delays and restrictions that they say damaged London’s credibility with allies.
HMS Dragon was facing issues with its "onboard water systems," which impacted water provisions for sailors on board, The Daily Mail first reported.
MULTIPLE ALLIES DECLINE US CALLS FOR STRAIT OF HORMUZ SUPPORT AMID RISING MIDDLE EAST TENSIONS

British Prime Minister Keir Starmer discusses defense spending and naval capabilities, pictured alongside the Type 45 destroyer HMS Dragon (D35) featuring its distinctive red dragon bow art. (Justin Tallis/AFP via Getty Images; Phby Jaimi Joy-WPA Pool/Getty Images)

"HMS Dragon is undertaking a routine logistics stop and a short maintenance period in the Eastern Mediterranean, allowing the ship to take onboard provisions, optimise systems and conduct maintenance," the Ministry of Defense said in a statement to the outlet.
The ministry said in a statement to the Daily Mail that if necessary the ship will be "able to sail at short notice."

"The UK continues to maintain a robust and layered defensive presence in the Eastern Mediterranean, working in coordination with allies. This includes Typhoon and F-35 jets, Wildcat and Merlin helicopters, and advanced counter-drone and air defence systems."

While the Iran war began on Feb. 28, the U.K. did not announce the deployment of HMS Dragon to protect its air bases in Cyprus until five days later. The announcement came a day after Iranian-backed militia group Hezbollah struck RAF Akrotiri, one of the United Kingdom's air bases in Cyprus. HMS Dragon did not depart from Portsmouth, England, until March 10 — a week after Starmer's announcement.
HEGSETH DECLARES 'DECISIVE MILITARY VICTORY' OVER IRAN

The Type 45 destroyer HMS Dragon is seen moored in the Royal Navy Dockyard on Oct. 28, 2024, in Portsmouth, England. Keir Starmer confirmed on March 3, 2026, that the HMS Dragon would be deployed in Cyprus. (Matt Cardy/Getty Images)

Trump and Starmer have been at odds since the conflict's onset. While the United Kingdom has allowed the U.S. military to operate out of those bases, Starmer restricted the U.S. military from carrying out offensive missions from its bases. Trump compared Starmer's approach to Iran to former United Kingdom Prime Minister Neville Chamberlain, who adopted an appeasement policy toward Nazi Germany during World War II.
During a press briefing, War Secretary Pete Hegseth on Wednesday called on "so-called allies," referring to the United Kingdom, to "take notes" on what the U.S. and Israel accomplished.

Criticism of Starmer's handling of Iran is also coming from United Kingdom Shadow Housing Secretary James Cleverly, a member of the Tory Party who is also a military reserve officer. Cleverly scrutinized Starmer's decision to visit the Middle East after the ceasefire was brokered in an interview with GB News.
FARAGE SLAMS BRITISH PRIME MINISTER FOR ‘EXTRAORDINARY’ LACK OF SUPPORT FOR TRUMP'S IRAN STRIKES
"He was opposing the United States using their own aircraft from British bases. Then he was in favor of it. He delayed the decision to deploy British naval assets," Cleverly said.
"He left British military personnel and our allies in the region not properly defended, and now he's finally engaging properly with this situation," Cleverly continued.
He claimed that Starmer's conduct had cost the country "credibility on the world stage."
\President Donald Trump meets with British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on July 28, 2025, in Turnberry, Scotland. (Christopher Furlong/Getty Images)

"I know a lot of our friends and allies in the region and beyond are very disappointed in Britain's response. And that is entirely because of decisions that Keir Starmer failed to make," Cleverly said.
British journalist Patrick Chrysty, host of GB News, also criticized the United Kingdom's efforts in the Iran war. He called Secretary of Defense John Healey a "bumbling idiot."

"It took us a month to get HMS Dragon to Cyprus after Iranian-backed terror group Hezbollah attacked our military base there... And right as the world holds its breath, HMS Dragon has a fault with its fresh water supply. It's gone to dock for repairs. It's out of action. This is an abomination!"
CLICK HERE TO DOWNLOAD THE FOX NEWS APP

John Hemmings, director of the National Security Centre at the Henry Jackson Society, said in a statement to Fox News Digital that Starmer's visit to the Gulf is his way of showing the United Kingdom is in support of Western allies' efforts in Iran.

"UK Prime Minister Starmer’s trip to the Persian Gulf shows the pressure he is under to ‘fly the flag’ and it’s clear that he’s trying to use Britain’s traditional networks and connections amongst the Gulf Arabs. In some ways, the Starmer team’s behind-the-scenes mediation strengths were proven in the Hamas-Israel peace deal with Jonathan Powell leading,
"This time, Yvette Cooper at the FCDO has been in the lead, running a virtual meeting of over 40 countries to coordinate a response to Iran’s blockade in early April."
Fox News Digital reached out to the U.K.'s Ministry of Defense for comment.
Elaine Mallon is a writer for Fox News Digital and Fox Business covering national politics.

title: Evangelical leaders rally for Trump and Israel as Operation Epic Fury reshapes the region
summary: Evangelical leaders say support for Israel has remained strong during Operation Epic Fury, the joint U.S.-Israel military campaign against Iran, as a new ceasefire takes hold.
story: Fox News senior strategic analyst Ret. Gen. Jack Keane breaks down President Donald Trump’s warning to Iran, efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and the rescue of a downed U.S. airman on ‘America’s Newsroom.’

Despite efforts to shift evangelical Christian support away from Israel, some of the most influential leaders say the community has stood firmly by the Jewish state since the start of the joint U.S.-Israel operation against Iran.

As Wednesday's ceasefire took effect, Dr. Mike Evans, founder of the Friends of Zion Heritage Center in Jerusalem and a close evangelical ally of President Donald Trump, reflected on the war. Evans flew to Israel on Feb. 26 as tensions escalated ahead of the operation.
During his visit, Evans toured impact sites caused by enemy missiles, met wounded civilians and Holocaust survivors, and provided $50,000 in financial assistance to a bereaved family.
"I knew the war would start, so I flew to the area. I have been in 41 wars. I go there intentionally when people are hurting to help them," Evans told Fox News Digital.
TRUMP VOWS TO HIT IRAN 'VERY HARD' AFTER OBLITERATING NEARLY '90 PERCENT' OF REGIME MISSILES
Rescue workers and military personnel carry the body of a victim after several people were killed by an Iranian missile strike in Beit Shemesh, Israel, March 1, 2026. (Leo Correa/AP Photo)

On March 1, an Iranian missile struck the city of Beit Shemesh, near Jerusalem, killing nine people. Evans arrived at the scene with first responders shortly after the strike. He later visited Jerusalem’s Hadassah Hospital, where he met Pnina Cohen, who was injured and lost both her husband and mother-in-law in the attack.
"I have been doing this for half a century. This is my life—combating antisemitism and helping the Jewish people," Evans said.

Operation Epic Fury, the joint U.S.-Israeli military campaign [named Roaring Lion in Israel], began on Feb. 28, with the stated goal of "obliterating Iran’s ballistic missile arsenal and production capacity," weakening its military infrastructure and preventing it from acquiring nuclear weapons, according to a White House briefing. The first day of the operation was marked by the killing of Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
AMERICAN WHO FLED IRAN SAYS CITIZENS 'DESPERATE' FOR FREEDOM, PRAYING FOR ISLAMIC REGIME TO FALL

Leading evangelical figures urge President Trump to continue backing Israel. (Graham; Tom Williams/CQ-Roll Call, Inc via Getty Images, Mike Evans; Lev Radin/Pacific Press/LightRocket via Getty Images)
According to Israel's Ministry of Health on Tuesday, since the beginning of the war, 7,183 people have been evacuated to hospitals, of whom 118 are currently hospitalized.

Evans described the U.S.-Israel partnership as unprecedented. "No one could have imagined an American president partnering so closely with Israel against radical Islam," he said, calling the campaign "historic."

He said evangelical support for Israel is rooted in religious belief. "The Bible is a Jewish book, and evangelicals believe in a Jewish person, Jesus," he said. "They see Israel as the biblical land and believe God keeps his promises."
The Evangelical Christian community, which numbers about 52 million people in the United States, supported Trump’s presidency on the condition that he would back Israel, Evans said.

Beyond political backing, Evans said evangelicals are active online. "We’ve had 127 million views on social media in the last eight weeks," he said. "We are fighting misinformation and antisemitism because lies can cost lives."
He emphasized that support is also practical. "We don’t just offer prayers — we provide financial help to those who lost homes and possessions."
President Donald Trump bids farewell to Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu as he leaves the White House after a meeting on April 7, 2025. (Alex Wong/Getty Images)

Evans acknowledged that a portion of younger evangelicals has shifted away from traditional support for Israel. "A segment has been influenced by universities and online voices," he said, estimating that about 22% to 23% have shifted. "We are working to reach them and I believe we can."

American Pastor John Hagee, the founder and chairman of the Christian Zionist organization Christians United for Israel, told Fox News Digital that the evangelical community supports Trump’s decision to seek the end of Iran’s menacing and murderous behavior.
AIRMAN RESCUE SHOWS U.S. CAN PENETRATE ENEMY TERRITORY 'ANYWHERE' IN IRAN, FORMER PENTAGON OFFICIAL WARNS

"We will be backing his request to Congress to fund this effort, and we will ensure our elected officials represent the will and morality of the American people by seeing this righteous endeavor to its righteous end," he said.

Hagee said that "as Americans, we have a right to defend ourselves against the Islamic Republic’s half-century of terror. As Christians, we are mandated to defend ourselves against evil, to stand with the oppressed against the same, and to stand with the children of Israel at all times."
People take shelter as Iran launched missiles and drones toward Israel following the U.S.-Israel attacks. (Mostafa Alkharouf/Anadolu via Getty Images)
"Evangelical Christians who’ve been raised in the church and are biblically literate are Christian Zionists," he said.

"The rise of antisemitism on the 'woke right' is not a product of evangelical churches, but rather a product of the false doctrine of Replacement Theology, repurposed and used as clickbait," he continued.

Any pastor or priest, politician or podcaster, who charges that the modern children of Israel are anything other than the direct descendants of Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, and the beneficiaries of God’s unbreakable covenant with Israel, Hagee said, is not preaching the word of God.
"Operation Epic Fury is making the world a safer and better place for all its inhabitants; stay the course, Mr. President," he said.
An Israeli fighter jet takes off for strikes in Tehran. (IDF)

Franklin Graham, president and CEO of Samaritan's Purse, an international, evangelical Christian disaster relief organization, said Iran has vowed to wipe the State of Israel off the face of the earth, and with nuclear weapons, they could.
"If President Trump had not stopped them, this is something this fanatical Islamic regime might have done within the next few months," he said.
The Rev. Franklin Graham, president and CEO of Samaritan's Purse, speaks during President Donald Trump's visit to Hurricane Helene-damaged North Carolina on Jan. 24, 2025. (Samaritan's Purse)

"My message to the American people would be to remember Israel is the only truly democratic nation in the Middle East — the only one. And they have been our nation’s closest ally in the region. I urge Americans to 'pray for the peace of Jerusalem' as the Bible instructs us," he continued.
CLICK HERE TO DOWNLOAD THE FOX NEWS APP
Graham said Trump stood with Israel in a way no other American president did in the past.

"We’ve never had a president like President Trump in my lifetime. If he says he’s going to do something, he’ll do it. He warned Iran that if it continued to develop nuclear weapons, the U.S. would intervene, and that’s exactly what he did."
Amelie Botbol is a freelance journalist based in Tel Aviv. Her articles have appeared in the New York Post, Canada’s National Post, and the Washington Times. Amelie can be followed on X @DatReporter

title: Rory McIlroy holds on to win his second straight Masters
summary: Rory McIlroy can breathe a sigh of relief
story: Rory McIlroy can breathe a sigh of relief.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

McIlroy won his second straight Masters Tournament on Sunday — joining Tiger Woods, Jack Nicklaus and Nick Faldo as the only golfers who’ve won the sport’s most prestigious tournament two years in a row.

McIlroy led by six strokes after 36 holes, but he entered Sunday tied for first after a disastrous third round. In danger of blowing the biggest two-round lead in Masters history, McIlroy rebounded under pressure, shooting a final-round 71 to secure his second green jacket.
Scottie Scheffler finished second. Cameron Young — who was in the final pairing with McIlroy — finished tied for third with three others, including last year’s runner-up, Justin Rose.

“I just can’t believe I waited 17 years to get one green jacket, and I get two in a row,” McIlroy said on the CBS telecast. “I think all of my my perseverance at this golf tournament over the years has really started to pay off."

title: Britney Spears voluntarily checks into treatment facility
summary: Britney Spears has voluntarily checked herself into a treatment facility, according to a representative for the pop star.
story: Britney Spears has voluntarily checked herself into a treatment facility, according to a representative for the pop star.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
The representative did not provide any other details about the decision, including where and why Spears plans to seek treatment.
TMZ first reported that she checked into a rehab facility in the U.S. for substance abuse, citing sources with direct knowledge.
Spears, 44, was arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence in March in Ventura County, California.

Following the arrest, a representative for Spears said that the incident was "completely inexcusable" and that "hopefully this can be the first step in long overdue change that needs to occur in Britney’s life."

There has been public concern for Spears over her frequent social media posts, many of which show her dancing alone in her California home, including one in which she was dancing with knives that she later said were not real.
She last posted one of those dancing videos Saturday.

In another post, on Friday, she shared a photo of herself. "When peeps try to make you larger than life ... tell them to BOW," the caption said. "If i ever showed them the fragility of my real heart."

Spears skyrocketed to fame in the late '90s and was widely considered the "Princess of Pop." But her career has been marred by high-profile incidents — including being photographed in 2007 with a freshly shaven head hitting a car with an umbrella.
In 2021, she was freed from her 13-year conservatorship, which had prevented her from making her own personal and financial decisions.
She shares two adult sons with her ex-husband Kevin Federline.

title: What to know about Trump's Iran blockade and the Strait of Hormuz
summary: The United States’ plan to blockade Iranian ports and marine traffic starting at 10 a.m. ET Monday has sent oil prices surging, as the countries reignited their tussle over the crucial Strait of Hormuz after peace talks ended without a deal
story: The United States’ plan to blockade Iranian ports and marine traffic starting at 10 a.m. ET Monday has sent oil prices surging, as the countries reignited their tussle over the crucial Strait of Hormuz after peace talks ended without a deal.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

American forces will begin enforcing the blockade after President Donald Trump on Sunday vowed to block “any and all Ships from trying to enter, or leave” the strait, which he had spent weeks pressuring Tehran to reopen.

The current two-week ceasefire came into force after Trump threatened to obliterate the “whole civilization” of Iran if it did not reopen the waterway, through which some 20% of the world’s oil passes.

The announcement of the blockade sent stocks falling and the price of crude oil soaring to above $100 amid questions around why the U.S. might cut off a trade route it had been trying to force open, how the blockade would work and what it could mean for countries across the world.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google
How will it work?

U.S. Central Command, or CENTCOM, clarified Trump’s Sunday comments, saying the blockade would only impact maritime traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports. It stressed that U.S. forces would not “impede freedom of navigation” for vessels transiting the strait to and from other ports.
CENTCOM said the blockade on the key trade route would be enforced “impartially” against any and all vessels entering or departing Iranian ports, including those on the Arabian Gulf and Gulf of Oman.

title: Europe celebrates as Viktor Orbán's landslide defeat deals a blow to Putin and Trump
summary: Leaders across Europe hailed the end of Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule in Hungary and victory of Peter Magyar's center-right Tisza Party as a blow to Russia's Vladimir Putin
story: LONDON —  “Europe! Europe!” was the chant ringing out from the banks of the Danube after an election upset that was celebrated across the continent Monday as a stunning defeat for those threatening its security.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

Presidents, prime ministers and European Union officials hailed the end of Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule in Hungary as a return to unity and shared values in the European Union, the 27-nation bloc that he criticized and sought to undermine.

Orbán, a close ally of Russia's Vladimir Putin and President Donald Trump, is viewed as a trailblazer for the global hard right. He conceded defeat Sunday night in what turned out to be a sweeping victory for challenger Peter Magyar and the center-right Tisza Party, which successfully united the country's opposition behind a vow to restore ties with Europe.
Tisza was set to win 138 seats in the 199-seat parliament, with almost all votes counted.
Why Hungary's election matters far beyond its borders
A succession of leaders called Magyar to offer their congratulations even before he took to the stage to address large crowds in the capital, Budapest, to declare victory.

Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk wrote on X: “Hungary, Poland, Europe, Back together! Glorious victory, dear friends!” He added “Ruszkik haza,” meaning “Russians go home” in Hungarian, a reference to Orban's backing for Putin and repeated veto on European support for Ukraine against Russia's invasion.
In a video clip posted by Tusk, the clearly delighted Polish leader is seen speaking to Magyar by phone and says "I think I'm happier than you."
Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez spoke for many on the continent when he posted: "Today Europe wins and European values win."

The European Democratic Party, a pan-European centrist group that supports greater integration across the continent, called Magyar's victory "a cry that shakes the conscience of us all" and posted a clip of crowds chanting "Europe! Europe!" at his victory rally in Budapest.
Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain — no longer a member of the E.U. but a close supporter of Ukraine — said: "This is an historic moment, not only for Hungary, but for European democracy."
The Kremlin, for its part, said Monday it hoped to continue its "pragmatic" ties with Hungary. “The Hungarians have made their choice. We respect this choice," spokesman Dmitry Peskov said.
Those celebrating the fall of Orbán should be mindful, analysts cautioned, that the huge influence he and his supporters built across the country’s institutions will not be easily removed.

“The structure of the last 16 years has been so entrenched and so much concerned with capturing all the commanding heights of the Hungarian state that it will take quite some time for anyone to unravel this,” said Jonathan Eyal, associated director of the Royal United Services Institute, a think tank in London.
Orbán, 62, is a key European ally of Trump whose populism and culture war posturing has provided a model for the MAGA movement.

In two decades at the helm of the Fidesz Party, he has staked out a reputation as a committed anti-immigration Christian nationalist who spoke of defending the West against what he framed as radical Islam and globalist forces.

The now former Hungarian leader has been widely criticized for dismantling or impairing many democratic institutions including the Constitution, law courts and the media, and packing influential committees with loyalists.

Tizsa is expected to have won at least two-thirds of the Hungarian parliament in the election, giving it a supermajority that means it can amend the Hungarian Constitution. "We will restore the system of checks and balances," Magyar told celebrating supporters Sunday.
He has vowed to unlock billions of dollars of in funding from the E.U. that had been halted due to repeated infringements and concerns about corruption and the democratic process.

But there are a series of official bodies stuffed with Orbán loyalists who have taken power away from Hungary's parliament to make policy, Eyal pointed out, as well as a powerful constitutional court full of his appointees.
"So sweeping away all these cobwebs is not something that could happen after a party on the Danube River," Eyal warned.
Magyar, a relatively young leader at 45, is a former member of Orbán's Fidesz Party and a former official in the Foreign Ministry — he only broke away to join Tisza in 2024.

Magyar is a traditionalist who support the hardline stance of the previous administration on immigration and has even signaled he may go further, by scrapping the country's guest worker scheme for non-E.U. citizens.

"Magyar is not exactly a dissident that has emerged out of pure thin air into into the new leader," Eyal said. "So there is, there is a legitimate question about how much of a break this is from someone who clearly shared some of the Orbán government's views for a lot of his political career."
Whatever happens, this result is blow to the regime in Moscow, Eyal added.
"Most Hungarian voters when they're asked, they say we don't want to be part of Russia, we want to be part of Europe," he said. "And so this is a very major lesson for far right."

title: Pope Leo says he has 'no fear' after Trump labels him 'weak' and 'terrible' in feud over Iran war
summary: Pope Leo XIV hit back Monday after President Donald Trump sharply criticized him as “WEAK on crime” and “terrible for Foreign Policy” after the pontiff's Iran war criticism.
story: ABOARD THE PAPAL PLANE — Pope Leo XIV hit back Monday after President Donald Trump sharply criticized the first American leader of the Catholic Church as “WEAK on crime” and “terrible for Foreign Policy.”
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

Leo, who has been unusually direct in his criticism of the U.S. and Israel's war on Iran, told NBC News that he had “no fear of the Trump administration” and vowed to keep up his appeals for peace that he said were rooted in the gospel.
Trump leveled his criticism of the pope in remarks to reporters and on social media.
“I don’t think he’s doing a very good job. He likes crime, I guess,” the president told reporters at Joint Base Andrews, Maryland, late Sunday.

“We don’t like a pope that’s going to say that it’s OK to have a nuclear weapon. We don’t want a pope that says crime is OK in our cities. I don’t like it. I’m not a big fan of Pope Leo,” Trump added.
He posted earlier on Truth Social that “Pope Leo is WEAK on Crime, and terrible for Foreign Policy."

“Leo should be thankful because, as everyone knows, he was a shocking surprise. He wasn’t on any list to be Pope, and was only put there by the Church because he was an American, and they thought that would be the best way to deal with President Donald J. Trump. If I wasn’t in the White House, Leo wouldn’t be in the Vatican,” Trump said.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google
The pope responded while en route Monday to Algeria for the first stop of an 11-day tour of four African nations.

Speaking aboard the papal plane, the pope said “I have no fear of the Trump administration, or speaking out loudly of the message of the gospel, which is what I believe I am here to do, what the church is here to do.”
He added: “We are not politicians, we don’t deal with foreign policy with the same perspective he might understand it, but I do believe in the message of the gospel, as a peacemaker.”

On the flight he told reporters that Algeria was ”a very special trip for me" as it was the home of  St. Augustine, the inspiration of Leo's religious spirituality. "He was a bridge of inter-religious dialogue, so we must continue to build bridges and reconciliation for all people,” the pope said.
Trump’s heavy criticism did not stop the pontiff from continuing to speak out against the war.

Upon his arrival in Algiers — the capital of a country ravaged in the past by war — the pope stopped by the monument to independence from French rule, and said that as conflicts continue to multiply throughout the world, we cannot add resentment upon resentment.

The pope last week criticized Trump’s public threats to “wipe out” Iranian civilization, saying “attacks on civilian infrastructure are against international law” and urging people to contact leaders and members of Congress to call for peace.

He has urged Trump to end the war in Iran, and he lamented in his Easter message last week that the world is “becoming indifferent” to violence. Leo had also criticized the Trump administration’s immigration policies.
In a brief statement Sunday night, Archbishop Paul Coakley, president of the U.S. Conference of Catholic Bishops, rebuked Trump's criticism of the pope.

“I am disheartened that the President chose to write such disparaging words about the Holy Father," Coakley wrote. "Pope Leo is not his rival; nor is the Pope a politician. He is the Vicar of Christ who speaks from the truth of the Gospel and for the care of souls.”
Trump separately posted a picture Sunday night portraying himself as having saint-like powers akin to those of Jesus Christ.

Wearing a biblical-style robe, Trump is seen laying hands on a bedridden man as light emanates from his fingers. A soldier, a nurse, a praying woman and a bearded man in a baseball cap all look on, with American landmarks in the background and the sky filled with eagles and an American flag.
Trump hasn't always been critical of Leo. At the time, Trump called the pope's election in May "an honor for our country."

"It’s such a great honor for our country to have an American pope. I mean, what greater honor could there be? That we were a little bit surprised, very happy. But just a great, absolutely great honor," he said at the time.

A March NBC News poll found U.S. voters viewed Pope Leo more favorably than Trump. Forty-two percent of respondents said they had positive views of the pope, while just 8% had negative views of him. While 41% of respondents said they had positive views of Trump, 53% said they had negative views of him.
Claudio Lavanga reported from aboard the papal plane, and Tara Prindiville and Raquell Coronell Uribe from Washington.

title: Rep. Eric Swalwell drops bid for California governor after sexual misconduct allegations
summary: The Democratic candidate faced immediate calls to drop out, with many Democrats withdrawing their endorsements.
story: Democratic Rep. Eric Swalwell said Sunday that he is dropping out of the race for governor after days of pressure from his close allies and staffers over multiple sexual misconduct allegations levied against him.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

"I am suspending my campaign for Governor," Swalwell said on X. "To my family, staff, friends, and supporters, I am deeply sorry for mistakes in judgment I’ve made in my past. I will fight the serious, false allegations that have been made — but that’s my fight, not a campaign’s."

His statement follows a torrent of criticism from both sides of the aisle after a former staffer alleged in a San Francisco Chronicle article published Friday in which the former staffer alleged she had sexual encounters with the congressman while she was working in his office and that he sexually assaulted her twice while she was too intoxicated to consent. NBC News has not verified her allegations, but it confirmed her identity and that she worked for Swalwell from 2019 until 2021.

The Chronicle says it corroborated the former staffer’s story by reviewing text messages she sent to a friend three days after one of the alleged encounters, with the woman saying in a text that she told Swalwell to stop. The paper also interviewed her boyfriend at the time, who confirmed that she told him about the alleged assault. In addition, the paper’s reporters viewed medical records that showed she sought pregnancy and STD tests afterward.
Swalwell told the Chronicle in a statement that the woman’s allegations were “false.”

CNN later reported on sexual misconduct allegations against Swalwell from four women, including one whose story matched details from the Chronicle article. One of the women, Democratic influencer Ally Sammarco, said Swalwell sent her unsolicited photos of his penis.

The news outlet said it corroborated the women’s accounts through interviews with family members and friends, in addition to reviewing messages Swalwell exchanged with the women. NBC News has not independently corroborated their stories.
Swalwell later posted a video on social media saying, “These allegations of sexual assault are flat false.”
“They did not happen. They have never happened. And I will fight them with everything I have,” Swalwell said in the video.
Still, Swalwell faced immediate calls from fellow Democrats to drop out of the race, including Reps. Jimmy Gomez and Adam Gray of California, the co-chairs of his campaign.
Rep. Nancy Pelosi, D-Calif., thr former House speaker who has been a close ally to Swalwell, said in a statement Friday that the allegations “must be respected and heard.”

“This extremely sensitive matter must be appropriately investigated with full transparency and accountability,” she said. “As I discussed with Congressman Swalwell, it is clear that is best done outside of a gubernatorial campaign.”

Democrats outside California also urged Swalwell to drop his bid. Sen. Ruben Gallego, D-Ariz., said he was retracting his endorsement, writing on social media that he read the Chronicle’s reporting and “takes it seriously.”

Fifty-five former congressional and campaign staffers who worked for Swalwell signed a public letter Sunday calling on him to drop his gubernatorial bid and resign from the House. They also urged law enforcement to open an investigation into the allegations from the former staffer who alleged he sexually assaulted her twice, saying, “No one is above the law.”
“We stand unequivocally with our colleague, who showed extraordinary courage in coming forward to share her truth. We believe her,” the letter read.
On Saturday, the Manhattan district attorney’s office in New York confirmed to NBC News that it was launching an investigation into allegations of sexual assault against Swalwell.
The primary election for governor of California is June 2, with early voting set to begin May 4.
Former Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Becerra, who is running for California governor, said in a video posted to X that Swalwell dropping out was "the right thing to do."
"Now we get to the business of accountability," he added.
San Jose Mayor Matt Mahan, another candidate for governor, said in a post to X that Swalwell is "done."
"He does not get any credit for doing less than the bare minimum," Mahan said. "Exiting a race you should never have entered deserves no credit."
Rusty Hicks, the chair of California's Democratic party, said in a statement that Swalwell "has finally done the right thing by ending his campaign for Governor."
"Swalwell's reprehensible actions confirmed he is unqualified and unfit to seek or hold public office in California," Hicks said.

Hicks confirmed that Swalwell's name would remain on the ballot because the deadline has passed to remove it, but called on Californians "to join me in ensuring all voters know he is unfit for public office and receives as little support as possible at the ballot box."

Swalwell has been near the top of every recent poll in the primary, often as the best-performing Democrat. But he and several other Democratic contenders, including billionaire Tom Steyer and former Rep. Katie Porter, have been locked in close competition.

All candidates run on the same primary ballot regardless of party in California, with the top two finishers advancing to the general election. Some Democrats have been concerned in recent weeks about the possibility of a split in the crowded field, allowing two Republicans to grab the general election slots. But President Donald Trump’s recent endorsement of a prominent Republican, Steve Hilton, changed the calculus.
Swalwell, who has served in Congress since 2013, has been a vocal critic of Trump’s and was one of the Democrats’ nine impeachment prosecutors during Trump’s trial following the Jan. 6 Capitol riot.

Swalwell was the subject of a two-year investigation into his ties to a 2014 campaign volunteer, Christina Fang, who later was suspected to be a Chinese spy. The House Ethics Committee took no action against Swalwell after that investigation, and he has repeatedly denied any wrongdoing.
Since Friday’s allegations, he has also faced calls for his resignation or expulsion from the House.
On Sunday, Rep Ro. Khanna, D-Calif., told Fox News, “I believe that he should resign,” adding that he’s also calling for a law enforcement investigation and a House Ethics Committee investigation.
Two other California Democratic lawmakers — Reps. Sam Liccardo and Jared Huffman — also called for Swalwell to resign from the House on Saturday.

“I’ve seen enough. With his nuanced statement aimed at defending likely criminal charges, Swalwell all but admits a per se abuse of power under House ethics rules: sex with a subordinate. He must now drop out of the Governor’s race and resign from Congress. Rep. Tony Gonzales, who admitted to the same violation, should also resign. If they don’t, I will support voting to expel both of them,” Huffman said in a post on X.

Gonzales, R-Texas, has been accused of exchanging sexually explicit messages with two former staffers, one of whom died by suicide. On a podcast in March, Gonzales admitted that he’d had an affair with a staffer, saying: “I made a mistake, and I had a lapse in judgment. And there was a lack of faith, and I take full responsibility for those actions.” He added that he had “reconciled” with his wife.

“Swalwell must halt his campaign, resign from office, and face the consequences of a full investigation. Democrats must not equivocate in the face of uncomfortable allegations about one of our own,” Liccardo wrote in a separate X post.
Rep. Anna Paulina Luna, R-Fla., vowed Saturday on X to bring a motion to the House floor to expel Swalwell. It would need a two-thirds vote in favor to pass.

Several House members, including Pramila Jayapal, D-Wash., have signaled openness to the idea of expelling Swalwell alongside GOP lawmakers who have faced sexual misconduct allegations, like Gonzales.
Asked Sunday whether she’d vote to expel Swalwell, Jayapal told NBC News’ “Meet the Press,” “And Congressman Gonzales, I would, I’ve already said that.”
Appearing earlier on the program, Rep. Byron Donalds, R-Fla., also said he’d vote to expel Gonzales and Swalwell.
“As far as I’m concerned, both gentlemen need to go home,” Donalds said.

title: Britney Spears voluntarily checks into treatment facility
summary: Britney Spears has voluntarily checked herself into a treatment facility, according to a representative for the pop star.
story: Britney Spears has voluntarily checked herself into a treatment facility, according to a representative for the pop star.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
The representative did not provide any other details about the decision, including where and why Spears plans to seek treatment.
TMZ first reported that she checked into a rehab facility in the U.S. for substance abuse, citing sources with direct knowledge.
Spears, 44, was arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence in March in Ventura County, California.

Following the arrest, a representative for Spears said that the incident was "completely inexcusable" and that "hopefully this can be the first step in long overdue change that needs to occur in Britney’s life."

There has been public concern for Spears over her frequent social media posts, many of which show her dancing alone in her California home, including one in which she was dancing with knives that she later said were not real.
She last posted one of those dancing videos Saturday.

In another post, on Friday, she shared a photo of herself. "When peeps try to make you larger than life ... tell them to BOW," the caption said. "If i ever showed them the fragility of my real heart."

Spears skyrocketed to fame in the late '90s and was widely considered the "Princess of Pop." But her career has been marred by high-profile incidents — including being photographed in 2007 with a freshly shaven head hitting a car with an umbrella.
In 2021, she was freed from her 13-year conservatorship, which had prevented her from making her own personal and financial decisions.
She shares two adult sons with her ex-husband Kevin Federline.

title: Rory McIlroy holds on to win his second straight Masters
summary: Rory McIlroy can breathe a sigh of relief
story: Rory McIlroy can breathe a sigh of relief.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

McIlroy won his second straight Masters Tournament on Sunday — joining Tiger Woods, Jack Nicklaus and Nick Faldo as the only golfers who’ve won the sport’s most prestigious tournament two years in a row.

McIlroy led by six strokes after 36 holes, but he entered Sunday tied for first after a disastrous third round. In danger of blowing the biggest two-round lead in Masters history, McIlroy rebounded under pressure, shooting a final-round 71 to secure his second green jacket.
Scottie Scheffler finished second. Cameron Young — who was in the final pairing with McIlroy — finished tied for third with three others, including last year’s runner-up, Justin Rose.

“I just can’t believe I waited 17 years to get one green jacket, and I get two in a row,” McIlroy said on the CBS telecast. “I think all of my my perseverance at this golf tournament over the years has really started to pay off."

title: ‘You, Me & Tuscany’ Stars and Creators Share Why Mario’s ‘Let Me Love You’ Is ‘The Anchor of This Movie’
summary: The "You, Me & Tuscany" stars, producer and director share why Mario's "Let Me Love You" was the best song for their film.
story: Warning: Spoilers ahead for “You, Me & Tuscany.”
There’s a moment in “You, Me & Tuscany” that transports audiences back to the early aughts, specifically 2004.

During one scene in the romantic comedy, Halle Bailey’s Anna gets into Regé-Jean Page’s Michael’s car and Mario’s Grammy-nominated hit “Let Me Love You” blasts through the stereo. Anna is befuddled at the reserved winemaker’s classic R&B selection.
“I just didn’t expect this from you,” she tells him.
Michael asks, “What does that mean?”
When she says that she pictured him as the type of guy who listens to podcasts, he quips, “So, boring?”
“Well, yes,” she nods.

In “You, Me & Tuscany,” Anna, who recently lost her job and residence, is inspired to book a trip to Italy after meeting a charming Italian man named Matteo (Lorenzo de Moor). Matteo happens to own a villa that is currently sitting empty — and, unbeknownst to him, is where Anna finds herself staying during her Tuscan getaway.

Once there, she meets the handsome Michael, who thinks she is his cousin Matteo’s fiancée after Anna’s caught living in the villa. In over her head as Matteo’s family starts to plan their wedding, Anna begins to develop feelings for Michael.

After the car ride scene, “Let Me Love You” continues to be part of the storyline when Matteo’s mom tells Anna about a family wedding tradition that includes a singing portion. She asks Michael to stand in for Matteo (who is nowhere in sight and has no idea Anna is in Italy) and he jokingly sings “Let Me Love You” to Anna. The song also plays an emotional and crucial part at the end of the film.

“There was always a musical moment in the script. What the song was going to be actually came in relatively late,” Page tells TODAY.com during a sit-down interview in Napa, California. “We just kind of wanted to find the right vibe, to find what worked between the characters, to find something that was both surprising and familiar. I think we landed somewhere really fun."
The actor notes that after watching the film, “People kept bringing it up. So I’m assuming that that’s a pretty fun takeaway.”
Page also jokes that singing in front of Bailey, a Grammy-nominated singer who is one-half of the duo Chloe x Halle and also a solo artist, was “terrifying.”
Bailey, on her end, applauds her co-star, saying, “He has a great voice.”
Producer Will Packer speaks more in-depth with TODAY.com about the inclusion of “Let Me Love You” and why that song was chosen.

“Mario’s song, ‘Let Me Love You’ is thematically, really, the anchor of this movie. And we needed something that would make our character, (Anna), feel like, ‘Oh my goodness, I’m so surprised (by) Mr. Rigid, stiff, hot-looking guy, British Regé, playing Michael. What’s he doing listening to this?!’” Packer explains. “We needed a song that has some flavor to it. We need a classic song that audiences would know.”
Director Kat Coiro adds that she loved that “Let Me Love You” is “about valuing a woman and having her recognize her worth, because that is part of the journey that Anna goes on.”
“She falls in love with herself. And that song, I think, really keys into how women want (and deserve) to be treated,” she says.
The team, they say, discussed a number of songs that would work for the film.
“But without a doubt, we landed on the perfect one,” Packer says. “We went through a lot of songs to figure out what would capture that same energy we’re talking about. Mario won.”

title: Offset Released From Hospital, Thanks People for Their Support After Being Shot in Florida
summary: Offset is recovering and on the mend after being hospitalized following a shooting in Florida.
story: Offset is recovering and on the mend after being hospitalized following a shooting in Florida.
On April 10, a spokesperson for the 34-year-old rapper confirmed to TODAY.com that “Offset has been released from the hospital and he is up and walking.”
“We’re incredibly grateful to the doctors, nurses, and the entire hospital staff who took such great care of him,” the spokesperson added.
Offset, whose given name is Kiari Cephus, was shot in the valet area outside of Seminole Hard Rock in Hollywood in Florida, around 7 p.m. on Monday, April 6, the Seminole Police Department said.
The artist, who shares three children with Cardi B, was taken to Memorial Regional Hospital with non-life-threatening injuries.
In an April 10 Instagram post, Offset also gave an update on his wellbeing. He also expressed his gratitude to those who have supported him during this time.
“Thank you to everyone who’s checked in on me and showed me love! I’m good….but I’m planning to be better!” the rapper wrote, adding that he’s focused on his family, his recovery and his music.
He wrote that he’s “realizing that life is made up of quiet wins and loud losses… Life’s a gamble and I’m still playing to win.”

That same night Offset was shot, rapper Lil Tjay was arrested and charged with disorderly conduct in connection with the shooting, police said. A second person was also detained at the scene but has not been charged, police spokesperson Gary Bitney told NBC News.
Lil Tjay's lawyer, Dawn M. Florio, denied the claims that he was involved in the shooting, calling the accusation “rumors.”

“Lil Tjay has not been shot, nor has Lil Tjay been charged with any shooting. Any reporting to the contrary is false,” she wrote on Instagram. “We encourage people to consult trusted news sources, and to verify the accuracy of any reporting, before reflexively sharing or repeating baseless rumors.”
Lil Tjay bonded out of jail Tuesday, April 7, where he briefly spoke with reporters saying, “I didn’t do no d--- fighting.”
Bitney added an “investigation to identify one or more additional persons involved in the incident is ongoing.”

title: 'You, Me & Tuscany' Stars Share the BTS Moment Made Them 'Suddenly Believe in Magic'
summary: "You, Me & Tuscany" stars Regé-Jean Page and Halle Bailey discuss their favorite BTS moment on set, building their chemistry and more.
story: It’s a blistering and gorgeous day in Napa, California, where “You, Me & Tuscany” stars Regé-Jean Page and Halle Bailey are conducting a handful of interviews to promote their romantic comedy set in central Italy.

The Elan Vineyards location with a terracotta villa and balcony that overlooks the countless rows of grapevines feels like a scene straight out of the Kat Coiro-directed and Will Packer-produced rom-com. “You, Me & Tuscany” explores the theme of a young woman finding herself, her purpose and falling love — all with a backdrop of the magnetic Italian landscape.
After landing the roles of Anna and Michael, Bailey and Page, respectively, first met at the 2025 Met Gala, bonding over the “intimidating” yet “quite magical evening,” Page told TODAY.com.
They “very quickly” formed a special trust between them, with Bailey adding that during the first couple of table reads in Italy they found their characters’ “heartbeats.”

In “You, Me & Tuscany,” a jobless Anna travels to Italy after meeting Matteo (Lorenzo de Moor), who happens to own an unoccupied villa as he travels for work. As seen in the trailer, Anna gets caught in a lie when she pretends to be Matteo’s fiancée and his family starts planning their wedding. Meanwhile Matteo has no clue this is going on.
Things get juicy after she meets Matteo’s cousin, Michael, and the two begin to fall for each other.

Anna’s journey as a young woman, unsure of what’s next, Bailey said, resonated with her. “I felt like I could see myself in her. I’m (26) now, and I feel like I’m learning so much in this year of my life, and I have a long way to go,” she said, quickly getting distracted and in awe as a butterfly flew between her and Page.
“That! That is why I signed on (to do this film), Page said, praising his co-star for her whimsical demeanor. “That happens when Halle is not acting, a sense of wonder and adventure.”
Bailey brings that wonder and adventure into her character, whose complicated yet comical road to self discovery helps Michael also find his path.

“They’re both kind of stuck in a rut. They’re both kind of not happy with where they are,” Page said. “And it turns out that the answer is love. The answer is you find someone to talk to. You find someone to see you in ways that you aren’t allowing you to see yourself.”
With the film's feel-good vibes, as well as the Tuscany location, Page and Bailey recalled the moments on set that made them realize this project was magical.
Coiro told TODAY.com that when directing the movie, she was adamant they film in Italy because “Tuscany is a part of the story. It is part of what she falls in love with.”
Bailey’s “personal favorite” moment was filming when Anna first meets Matteo’s Costa family and Michael arrives.
“It’s like, ‘What are you doing here?!’ I just love that whole moment and the slight awkwardness of, ‘What am I going to say now?’” she said. “And her getting deeper and deeper into this lie.”
Page recalled a day when they were shooting over a real Tuscan sunset that filled them with joy and gratitude.

“There was this moment between takes where suddenly the whole sky went purple and pink and blue, and the entire crew just stopped,” he said. “Everyone gets their phones out, everyone starts taking pictures with each other. Everyone just looks out over the hillside as the sun sets and there’s a feeling that comes over the crew where everyone just suddenly believes in magic.”
The moment, he said, made them all “pause for a second and take in” where they were and the opportunity at hand.

“And that, to me, felt like the purpose of shooting the movie there. It was to bottle just a piece of that and take it home and put it on a big screen,” he said. “Those moments are very rare. They’re a large part of why I do the job in the first place.”
“You, Me & Tuscany” is now in theaters.

title: Why Lo Bosworth Missed the 'Laguna Beach' Reunion
summary: Lo Bosworth Natale wasn't in the Laguna Beach reunion for a pretty good reason: She was pregnant with her first child.
story: Lo Bosworth Natale shot to stardom more than 20 years ago as a cast member on "Laguna Beach," but the reality star-turned-entrepreneur wasn't able to join her former cast members in person for “The Reunion: Laguna Beach."

"I was very pregnant when they shot the reunion," Bosworth Natale, who welcomed their first child, a daughter named Nelle, with husband Dom Natale in January, revealed during an April 10 visit to TODAY.
"Laguna Beach," which debuted on MTV in 2004, introduced viewers to to a group of real-life high school students in Laguna Beach, California.

For the show's reunion, which premiered April 10 on Roku, Bosworth Natale met up with former cast member Lauren Conrad to shoot scenes inside the offices of Bosworth Natale's women's wellness company, Love Wellness.

Bosworth Natale also joined Conrad, Stephen Colletti, Kristin Cavallari and other cast members over video to reminisce about their teenage dramas, which included no shortage of platonic dustups and romances.
"I think everybody was very nervous, actually, to get together but it turned out to be very healing," said Bosworth Natale. "Everyone keeps saying, oh, we feel so good after doing the reunion."
"It was actually really great. I hadn't seen a lot of the people in many, many years," she added.
The reunion was especially meaningful for Bosworth Natale, who missed her "real" high school reunion because she was at home doing "an IVF round."
"So, it was a true reunion for me to be able to get together with everybody," she noted.

During her visit to TODAY, Bosworth Natale also recounted how she and Conrad initially caught the attention of the series' producers, who were touring their high school's area in search of potential cast members.
She and Conrad, she recalled, were wearing “matching Juicy tracks suits” and sporting similar “crazy blond hair."
"Lauren and I were actually ditching class and we had to walk past the (administration) office to get to our car to go to wherever it was that we were going," she said.
"I accidentally set off my car alarm in front of the office as the producers were walking up the street," she said, laughing.
"It just was fate, I suppose," she added.
Being a star on "Laguna Beach" turned out to be a "life-changing' experience, she said.
"So many of us have had such success as a result of the show with different businesses and amazing families," she said. "It's just very heartwarming for me to go back and see the reunion."
"It's a beautiful time capsule for everybody," she added.
Welcoming her first child earlier this year so "fundamentally" changed Bosworth Natale, she decided to launch her own motherhood podcast "Tell Me I’m a Good Mom."
"Moms are tough and moms are great and moms deserve their own stage to be applauded and that's what I'm doing with the new podcast," she said.
Bosworth Natale announced last month on Instagram that she didn't attend the "Laguna Beach" reunion in person because she'd become a new mom.

“I didn’t go!!!!,” she wrote. “But not bc I didn’t want to — I did! I live in NYC and I just had a babbbyyyy and I have to have a fragment of placenta removed in a few days. I wore my black and white outfit at home yesterday in solidarity lol.”

title: Where ‘Euphoria’ Left All Its Main Characters Off After Season 2
summary: "Euphoria" Season 3 premieres Sunday, April 12, on HBO. Here's where Season 2 left Rue, Jules, Maddy, Nate, Cassie and more.
story: “Guilty or innocent?” asks "Euphoria's" Season 1 closing song, “All For Us,” by Zendaya and Labrinth — a question still hanging over its protagonists.
It’s been four years since the show's neon hues and glitter makeup last lit up our screens. Season 3 premieres Sunday, April 12, on HBO, reuniting viewers with East Highland’s chaos-prone cohort.
Season 3's trailer previews a significant time jump and flashes of what’s ahead: Rue in Mexico, Cassie in a wedding gown, Jules in New York, Lexi in Hollywood. But how did we get here?
Before we reunite with this tangled web of toxic friendships and fractured relationships, here’s where we left off.
Rue’s Relapse

At the center of the show is Rue, played by Zendaya, and her struggle with drug addiction. Rue’s sobriety was always fragile, but Season 2 shattered any illusion of stability. After relapsing, initially using at a party with Dominic Fike’s character, Elliot, she spiraled into one of the show’s most harrowing arcs, burning nearly every bridge in her life.

Most ominously, there’s Laurie (Martha Kelly), the drug dealer who fronted Rue a suitcase of drugs with the expectation she’d sell them and repay the debt, with interest. Unfortunately for Rue, her mom flushed the stash during an intervention, leaving Rue with nothing to pay Laurie back.
With Season 3's trailer placing Rue in Mexico, it’s hard not to wonder if she’s still trying to outrun or finally settle that debt.
Cal Jacobs’ Arrest
Cal Jacobs is played by Eric Dane, who filmed Season 3 amid his diagnosis with ALS before his death in February at age 53.

Cal, the father of Jacob Elordi's Nate, spent years living a carefully concealed double life, secretly recording his sexual encounters with men and transgender women while repressing his own identity. That facade finally cracked in a drunken, unfiltered confession to his family that exposed not just his actions, but his true self.

At the end of Season 2, Cal was arrested after Nate turned him into the police. But that doesn’t neatly close the chapter. His relationship with Nate is in ruins, and his history with Jules (Hunter Schafer) lingers in ways that are difficult to untangle.
And if the Season 3 trailer is any indication, his history with Jules is far from settled.
Lexi’s Play

After being sidelined most of Season 1, Maude Apatow’s character, Lexi, stepped into her own voice in the most recent season. She wrote, directed and performed a school play drawn directly from her and her sister Cassie's (Sydney Sweeney) lives, staging thinly veiled versions of their friends' most private moments for an audience of their classmates.

For the people being portrayed onstage, it wasn’t hard to recognize themselves. Rue saw a version of herself struggle with drugs through Lexi’s empathetic gaze, while Cassie saw something far less forgiving, fueling an explosive onstage fight between the sisters. What Lexi framed as honesty landed, for many involved, as exposure.
Ashtray's Death

Fezco (Angus Cloud) experienced tragedy at the very end of "Euphoria" Season 2. A police raid of the drug dealer's house resulted in his pseudo little brother Ashtray’s (Javon “Wanna” Walton) death and Fez’s arrest, cutting short one of the show’s most unexpectedly tender arcs: his connection with Lexi.
Angus Cloud died in 2023 at age 25 of an accidental overdose. His character Fez’s story now stands as one of the show’s most unresolved.
The 'Euphoria' Love Triangles
In addition to plenty of plot twists and action, "Euphoria" also features a complex web of romances and hookups. Here's where Season 2 left off these relationships:

Perhaps the most explosive fallout from Lexi’s play was the physical altercation between Maddy (Alexa Demie) and her best friend Cassie, though the fuse had already been lit. Rue accidentally exposed Cassie's relationship with Maddy's ex-boyfriend Nate in the penultimate episode, and by the time Lexi’s play aired it out for all to see, the finale's confrontation was inevitable.

And yet, Season 3's trailer complicates everything: Cassie and Nate appear to make it all the way to the altar, while Maddy somehow finds her way back into Cassie’s orbit. If anything, it suggests the triangle isn’t resolved so much as reshaped.

Rue and Jules’ relationship has always been defined by intensity and instability, but the introduction of Elliot in Season 2 complicated things further. Rue relapsed and hid it from Jules, often using with Elliot behind her back, while Elliot inserted himself into their dynamic in ways that blurred emotional and physical boundaries.
The breaking point came when Elliot told Jules about Rue’s drug use, which was portrayed by her friends as an intervention but seen by Rue as betrayal.
By the end of the season, trust was broken on all sides. The intervention set off Rue so much, she broke up with Jules.
"You and me, we're f---ing done. There's nothing f---ing there anymore," Rue yelled at a crying Jules.
Elliot is left in the middle, both a catalyst for the pair's fallout and a casualty of it.
Jules’ entanglement with both Nate and Cal sits at the intersection of some of "Euphoria’s" darkest themes: control, exploitation, and secrecy.
Jules and Cal first crossed paths on a hookup app, leading to a clandestine encounter Cal secretly recorded. One of many he documented without his partners' knowledge.

That history would later collide when Nate manipulates Jules — catfishing her and blackmailing her with the footage — and turns his father's secrets into leverage. Even with Cal arrested, the psychological grip of this triangle doesn’t disappear overnight.

title: Amanda Batula Apologizes to 'Everyone I've Disappointed,' Confirms She'll Attend 'Summer House' Reunion
summary: "Summer House" star Amanda Batula is issuing a sweeping apology to "everyone" she's "disappointed and hurt" following confirmation that she and co-star West Wilson are dating."Hi guys, I’ll keep this short and sweet.
story: "Summer House" star Amanda Batula is issuing a sweeping apology to "everyone" she's "disappointed and hurt" following confirmation that she and co-star West Wilson are dating.

"Hi guys, I’ll keep this short and sweet. I’ve obviously been MIA, but I wanted to come back on and say that I’m truly sorry to everyone I’ve disappointed and hurt, especially those I know personally who I’ve reached out to individually," Batula wrote April 10 in a statement in her Instagram story.

“For the sake of my mental health,” she continued, “I’m going to try to start living life with some sense of normalcy. If you see me out or posting online, please know that this still weighs very heavily on me. I’m not ignoring what’s happened or what’s unfolded.”
Batula added that she would attend the show's upcoming Season 10 reunion, where she would be “addressing any and all questions honestly and directly there.”
Batula and Wilson shocked fans when they confirmed their romance in a joint statement March 31, a little over two months after Batula announced her split from estranged husband Kyle Cooke in January.
Batula's close friend and co-star, Ciara Miller, was previously in an on-and-off romance with Wilson.
"We’ve seen the growing online speculation, so while this is still very new, we wanted to provide some clarity,” read the statement, which both Batula and Wilson shared on social media.

“It was never our intention to purposely hide anything. Given the complicated relationship dynamics involved and the scrutiny that comes with being on a reality show, we needed a little space to process things privately before speaking on it,” they continued.
The couple went on to say their “connection” grew out of a “genuine, longstanding friendship.”
Miller recently posed a pointed question about hooking up with a friend's ex-boyfriend while interviewing Sydney Sweeney at the “Euphoria” Season 3 premiere on April 7.
“I mean, if you steal your friend’s ex-boyfriend ...” Miller asked Sweeney on the red carpet.
“It’s not good,” Sweeney said.

(Granted, Sweeney is involved in a a similar storyline on “Euphoria,” which returns to HBO Max on April 12. Sweeney’s character, Cassie, secretly hooked up with Nate (Jacob Elordi), the ex of her best friend Maddy (Alexa Demie), in Season 2 of the drama.)

At the "Euphoria" premiere, Miller went on to ask Sweeney, “What do you think is the next step? I feel like you ought to get married,” seemingly referencing the Season 3 trailer for “Euphoria," which shows Cassie walking down the aisle.
“I mean, I would hope so. You got to make it worth it,” Sweeney responded.
“You got to make it worth it,” Miller said, before turning and looking directly at the camera.
Cooke also addressed his ex Batula's new romance with Wilson during an April 1 interview for the “So Bad It’s Good With Ryan Bailey” podcast.
When host Ryan Bailey asked Cooke how he was doing, the reality star responded, “I’m good. You know, I appreciate people checking in. I’m actually really worried about Amanda if I’m being honest.”
Cooke said he'd spoken with Batula, whom he said had "mental health issues," on the nights before and after she and Wilson released their statements confirming their romance.
"I understand people have all sorts of opinions, and I’m not justifying any behavior. But like, from what I’m seeing, she’s kind of getting cyber bullied,” Cooke said.

Cooke went on to say Wilson "seems to be the kind of guy playing multiple women at the same time." He added Wilson had reached out to him in  “some half-a---ed text message" that he compared to a "word salad."
Cooke added that he was now questioning the timeline of Batula and Wilson's romance.

“It definitely makes me second guess a lot of things last summer, at a minimum," he said. "Just how checked out she was. In my own unsuccessful ways, I was still trying to put an effort in. I mean, it’s a mental mind f--- for me.”
"I’m still trying to figure out the details. But I know I can imagine West is using the same charm that he’s used on just about every other chick," he added.

title: 'Laguna Beach' Cast Now: What Happened After the MTV Show Ended?
summary: Where is the Laguna Beach cast now? They're reuniting for a reunion on April 10. Read on for an update about their lives now.
story: Nearly 20 years after "Laguna Beach" aired its last episode, the cast of the MTV reality series is getting back together for a reunion.
On April 10, "The Reunion: Laguna Beach" will air on the Roku Channel.

Several members of the original cast will appear in the reunion special, including Lauren Conrad, Stephen Colletti, Kristin Cavallari, Trey Phillips, Talan Torriero, Christina Schuller, Lo Bosworth, Dieter Schmitz, Jessica Smith, Alex Hooser and Morgan Olsen.
Before the reunion premieres, we're sharing an update on what some of the cast members have been up to over the last two decades.
Kristin Cavallari
"Laguna Beach" viewers were always Team Lauren or Team Kristin in the Kristin-Lauren-Stephen love triangle.
In 2022, Kristin Cavallari and Stephen Colletti opened up about their enduring friendship in an episode of their “Laguna Beach” rewatch podcast, “Back to the Beach with Kristin and Stephen.”
“We were great. I mean, I would say you were my first love. Like, we really had a very sweet, great relationship,” Cavallari said.
In the episode, Cavallari revealed that she and Colletti have "stayed in touch over the years" and said they "reconnected a little bit more in the last year and a half."

After "Laguna Beach" ended, Cavallari appeared on 24 episodes of the show's spinoff series, "The Hills," per IMDB. Later, she also starred in an E! reality series called “Very Cavallari” and a docuseries called "Honestly Cavallari: The Headline Tour."

Cavallari has made guest appearances in several scripted TV series over the years, including "9-1-1:Nashville," "The League," "The Middle" and "CSI: NY." She also competed in Season 13 of "Dancing With the Stars."
In 2016, Cavallari published a book titled, "Balancing in Heels: My Journey to Health, Happiness, and Making It All Work." She's also the author of three cookbooks.
Cavallari married football player Jay Cutler in 2013. The couple went on to have three children. In 2020, the couple announced that they were getting a divorce.
These days, Cavallari keeps busy with her podcast "Let’s Be Honest with Kristin Cavallari."
In 2025, Cavallari appeared at the Shop TODAY Stages event and spoke about her work as an entrepreneur and her successful lifestyle brand, Uncommon James.
Lauren Conrad

"Laguna Beach" may have pit Lauren Conrad and Kristin Cavallari against each other, but the former rivals cleared the air in 2022 during an episode of the “Back to the Beach with Kristin and Stephen” podcast.

“My take on it is, you and I really never had any beef, really” Cavallari told Conrad. “Obviously, there was a little truth to what happened with the three of us. I felt like MTV coming kept it alive and made it way worse than it ever would have been.”
“Yeah, I think it was done when they started filming,” Conrad said.

Later on, Cavallari said they "never had an issue" with each other, but Conrad said, “I mean, we had an issue with each other. But it had been squashed. They ran with it. It made for an interesting show.”
They spoke more about their feud in an interview with TODAY.com ahead of the premiere.
“Obviously, it’s been so long. I mean, I’m just happy that we’ve gotten this time together where we can truly just put everything in the past. It’s been really nice,” Cavallari said.

After the end of "Laguna Beach," Conrad starred in the spinoff series, "The Hills," which followed her as she attended FIT and pursued a career in the fashion industry. The show also highlighted her internships with Teen Vogue and People's Revolution.

Today, Conrad is the designer behind the womenswear line LC Lauren Conrad and the children's line Little Co. In 2013, Conrad and her friend Hannah Skvarla founded a nonprofit online fair trade shop called The Little Market.
In 2014, Conrad married former musician, William Tell, who was part of the band Something Corporate and now works as a lawyer. The couple went on to welcome two sons.
Lo Bosworth

During her "Laguna Beach" days, Lo Bosworth was pretty tight with Lauren Conrad. In 2024, Bosworth told E! News she was still in touch with her high school pal but said she's “not in constant touch with a ton of people from the show.”

In a recent post on her Instagram page, Bosworth reflected on her time on the reality series, writing, "I was 16, had no idea what I was doing, and somehow that became the start of a semi public life that I’ve been navigating ever since."
After wrapping up "Laguna Beach," Bosworth appeared in the show's spinoff series, "The Hills."

Bosworth attended the University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) then moved to New York City, where she started a cooking and lifestyle blog. In a recent Instagram post, she explained that she had "severe vitamin deficiencies and was seeking answers" at the time.
Bosworth later created Love Wellness, a women's wellness brand that's focused on vaginal health, hormones, gut health and mental health.
"Took about 10 years, a lot of tears, and more product development meetings than I can count. I love my job!!!" she wrote on Instagram.
In July 2025, Bosworth married businessman Dom Natale. The couple welcomed their first child together, a daughter named Nelle, in January 2026.
Bosworth currently has a podcast called "Tell Me I'm a Good Mom" and a Substack newsletter about wellness.
Stephen Colletti
Stephen Colletti was known as the heartthrob of the group and was involved in a love triangle with Conrad and Cavallari during his "Laguna Beach" years.
In a recent interview with Entertainment Tonight, Colletti reflected on some of his romantic escapades from his younger years.
“I made some decisions back in the day that I’m not proud of and and we’ve had a lot of growth since then,” he said.
Overall, Colletti said the show’s producers “took their liberties” and dramatized the love triangle aspect of the show.

"When it comes to the love triangle, from the jump, they made it seem like Lauren and I were just straight up having this affair out in the open when I never cheated on Kristen during our relationship. Lauren and I never did anything like that," he said.

Following his time on "Laguna Beach," Colletti starred as Chase in "One Tree Hill" and appeared in 57 episodes. Since then, he's built up several other acting credits in projects like the TV series "Everyone Is Doing Great," the TV movie "Hometown Christmas" and several others.
Colletti also appeared in Season 4 of the Peacock reality series "Traitors."
In his personal life, Colletti is currently expecting his first child with his wife, NASCAR host and reporter Alex Weaver. The couple got married in October 2025.
Morgan Smith
After "Laguna Beach," Morgan Smith (formerly Olsen) pursued a career in the fashion industry like several of her former co-stars.
Smith, who now lives in South Carolina with her husband and three children, has a clothing brand named minnow that makes clothes for women, men and children.
The brand's website says minnow is "a reflection of the life and style of its founder, Morgan Smith, a mother and entrepreneur with a background in premium product marketing."
Per the website, Smith previously worked in both Los Angeles and New York.
Christina Sinclair
Christina Sinclair (formerly Schuller) spoke to Time in 2014 to discuss the experience of signing up for "Laguna Beach."

"We’re sitting there in class, and over the daily announcements they announce, ‘OK everyone, MTV’s here. They’re going to cast a reality show on Laguna Beach. If you’re interested, sign up in the quad at break.’ And I think for any 18 year old, that’s a dream come true," she said.
After "Laguna Beach," she attended the University of Southern California and moved back to her hometown to teach fitness classes.
Per Sinclair’s website, she works as a certified fitness instructor who specializes in barre fitness. In 2012, she was ranked a top 20 barre instructor in Los Angeles.
Sinclair is married to her husband, Chad, and the couple has three children.
Jason Wahler
Jason Wahler, who was known for his rocky relationship with Conrad, didn't have a stellar reputation when he appeared on "Laguna Beach" and "The Hills."
While struggling with substance abuse, Wahler appeared on “Celebrity Rehab with Dr. Drew” in 2010.
In 2016, Wahler opened up to Entertainment Tonight about his addiction journey, noting that he particularly struggled with alcohol and cocaine.
"I became a totally different person active in my addiction versus when I’m sober and living life in recovery," he said.
Wahler was arrested several times and sought treatment at multiple rehab centers over the years. He told Entertainment Tonight he was inspired to really change his life after attempting suicide.
"My addiction drove me to suicide," he said. "Not contemplation -- actually attempting suicide -- and somebody found me and that’s why I’m still here today. I’m very grateful for that."
Wahler married his girlfriend Ashley Slack in 2013. The couple now has three children together.
Since starting his recovery journey, Wahler has been transparent about the highs and lows he's encountered over time, including a relapse in 2018.

Wahler, who appeared on "The Hills: New Beginnings," describes himself as a "passionate advocate for addiction education and prevention" on his website. He works as a guest speaker and recovery advocate.
Wahler is currently the director of the Change Your Brain Foundation and is on the board of Cure Addiction Now.
Trey Phillips
Along with several of his classmates, Trey Phillips appeared on "Laguna Beach" as a high school student.
When Phillips was a guest on Colletti and Cavallari's podcast in 2022, fans of "Laguna Beach" learned that he's kept in touch with Colletti since filming wrapped many years ago.
Cavallari also complimented Phillips on how put together he was while they were starring in the show, saying, "You were so mature and so wise beyond your years."

After high school, Phillips attended Bard College to study human rights. He later dropped out to pursue a career in fashion and tried to create his own hat and sunglasses companies. When those failed, he went on to study fashion and later scored a job as a designer for Vera Wang, a company he worked at for seven years.
Later, Phillips relocated to London to continue his fashion career.
Talan Torriero
In "Laguna Beach," Talan Torriero was known as a bit of a Casanova and was romantically linked to Cavallari at times. But he told E! News in 2022 that he was really a "hopeless romantic."
"I loved being in relationships," he said.
In a recent interview with Entertainment Tonight, Torriero confirmed that he dated singer Nicole Scherzinger in the early 2000s.
"She was a big part of my life. I’m really happy to see all the success she’s had," he said.
Torriero got married to his wife Danielle in 2014. The couple shares three children.

"I think that young Talon had a good time. He had a lot of fun. Talon today is boring. He’s married. He’s got three kids. He’s got basketball practice on Saturday, so he’s got to get home tomorrow," he recently told Entertainment Tonight.
Dieter Schmitz
In a recent interview with Entertainment Tonight, Schmitz described what made the reality show so popular, saying the group had been friends since the first grade.
“I think that’s what made the show special,” he said.
Looking back on his experience of filming a reality show, Schmitz joked that he has one regret.
"I said a lot of bad words and they didn't use beeps, so I would just go quiet. And my mom was like, 'Your mic always goes out,'" he teased.
Life has changed a lot for Dieter Schmitz since his "Laguna Beach" days. On his Instagram page, the former reality star describes himself as a "proud papa of two," a "husband" and a "hotelier."
When Schmitz got married in 2016, Colletti served as a groomsman.
During his interview with Entertainment Tonight, Schmitz said he typically doesn't bring up his time on reality TV at work, but noted that the reunion had "brought it back a little bit."
"All my coworkers that I hadn’t told, they’re like, 'What is happening?'" he said.
Jessica Evans

Jessica Evans (formerly Smith) appears in the "Laguna Beach" reunion special. During an interview with Entertainment Tonight, the former reality star said she still keeps in touch with Cavallari, Torriero and Olsen.
“We’ve got our little fab four group chat that we text on all the time so that’s great. We’re all very close. We were all in the same graduating class as well,” she said.
Evans, who has been married almost 17 years, said her husband hasn't watched "Laguna Beach."
“He’s just not a reality TV guy,” she said.
However, a few of her four children may be interested in seeing the show.

"I told my kids that they could watch it when they are the age that I was when I was on it. So my oldest is 15, which is how old I was. He has no interest. My 13-year-old, who is here with me right now, enlightens me today that she’s watched Season 1 at the neighbor’s house. I was like, 'Great. Well, better than Season 2, right?' I was like, 'Don’t go into Season 2 yet. Wait for me for that,'" she said.
Alex Hooser
